text: I envision my ideal life having a central "home base" partner or anchor, rather than keeping my living and financial life entirely my own.
My primary drive for seeking connections is the pursuit of sexual variety and erotic thrill, rather than building deep emotional romance.
I prefer to keep different connections strictly compartmentalized in their own lanes, rather than letting them cross-pollinate into one integrated web.
I prefer to find and manage connections through directed design (apps, explicit boundaries, scheduled quests), rather than letting them erupt organically from permissive social circles.
I envision my ideal life having a central "home base" partner or anchor, rather than keeping my living and financial life entirely my own.
I believe it is natural and ethical to organize my life around a prioritized relational core, rather than maintaining my individual sovereignty above all else.
I imagine wanting to deeply merge my logistics (finances, housing, family building) with a partner, rather than maintaining strict logistical independence.
I envision my ideal relationship architecture having a central, prioritized partnership at its core, rather than keeping all connections on an equal, peripheral level.
I see the "couple/unit" as the fundamental organizing unit of my relational life, rather than the "individual."
My primary drive for seeking connections is the pursuit of sexual variety and erotic thrill, rather than building deep emotional romance.
I view sexual play primarily as a recreational enhancement to life, rather than a path to building deeply intertwined emotional lives.
I am drawn to erotic energy primarily for the physical thrill and sport of it, rather than for the purpose of cultivating a chosen family.
I believe connections are best when they serve the purpose of mutual pleasure and play, rather than needing to serve the practical realities of life.
I imagine I would be more fulfilled by a wide variety of casual, playful sexual connections than by a few deeply emotionally integrated romances.
I prefer to keep different connections (romantic, sexual, social) strictly compartmentalized in their own lanes, rather than letting them cross-pollinate into one integrated web.
I believe a protected, siloed approach to connections is essential, rather than letting them integrate into my wider social or logistical fabric.
I prefer that casual or play partners remain separate from my core life and friend groups, rather than bringing them "into the light" as integrated community members.
I value keeping boundaries firm so connections do not reshape my relational system, rather than allowing people to freely become family or merge logistics.
I view the boundary of my relational system as a wall that protects my core, rather than a porous membrane that lets connections freely enter and integrate.
I prefer to find and manage connections through directed design (apps, explicit boundaries, scheduled quests), rather than letting them erupt organically from permissive social circles.
I prefer to rely on explicitly negotiated rules and boundaries regarding interactions, rather than fluid, unspoken social vibes.
I believe relational structures work best as a scheduled, defined activity, rather than something that spontaneously happens when the vibe is right.
I prefer relationships to stay true to their original intent (e.g., a play partner stays a play partner), rather than letting them organically evolve far beyond their starting point.
I am someone who designs my relationship structure from a blueprint, rather than letting it grow like a garden based on who we meet and what the situation requires.
